title: Out of bounds access with Distributed table on partitioned table
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce, although it's not critical. Partitioning by the same column twice is a bad idea.
### How to reproduce
With `test_shard_localhost` setup from testing run:
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 String, c1 Int) ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY (c1, c1, SHA512_256(c0)) ORDER BY tuple();
CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 String, c1 Int) ENGINE = Distributed('test_shard_localhost', default, t0);
INSERT INTO TABLE t1 (c0, c1) VALUES ('1', 1);
SELECT c1 FROM t1; --out of bounds
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/vector:1436: assertion __n < size() failed: vector[] index out of bounds

poc: CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 String, c1 Int) ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY (c1, c1, SHA512_256(c0)) ORDER BY tuple();
CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 String, c1 Int) ENGINE = Distributed('test_shard_localhost', default, t0);
INSERT INTO TABLE t1 (c0, c1) VALUES ('1', 1);
key_bug_features: Partitioning by the same column twice in a table
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (c0 String, c1 Int, c2 Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY (c1, c1, c2) ORDER BY (c0, c1);
CREATE TABLE t3 (c0 String, c1 Int) ENGINE = Distributed('test_shard_localhost', default, t2);
INSERT INTO t2 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES ('test', 10, 3.14);
SELECT c1 FROM t3 WHERE c1 = 20;
